ddls:
CREATE TABLE "content" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of content */,
  "date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the content was aggregated */,
  "feed_type" TEXT /* Type of content feed (e.g., work, inspiration) */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the content image */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text summary or description of the content */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the original source of the content */,
  "via" TEXT /* Intermediate source through which content was aggregated, if any */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the content */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the content */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Category of the content */,
  "engagement_score" INTEGER /* Score representing user engagement with the content */,
  "is_featured" INTEGER /* Indicates if the content is featured */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the content */,
  "is_summarized" INTEGER /* Indicates if the content has a summary */,
  "summary_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding summary */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_source_url FOREIGN KEY ("source_url") REFERENCES content_sources ("source_url"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_summary_id FOREIGN KEY ("summary_id") REFERENCES content_summaries ("summary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES content_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., regular user, admin, content curator) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* A brief biography of the user */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date the user registered */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Last date the user was active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "feed_type" TEXT /* Type of content feed the user is interested in */,
  "interest_level" INTEGER /* Level of interest in the feed type (e.g., 1-10 scale) */,
  "preference_score" INTEGER /* Score representing the user's preference level for the category */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the preference is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_feed_type FOREIGN KEY ("feed_type") REFERENCES content_categories ("category_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the content category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL of the category icon */,
  "is_popular" INTEGER /* Indicates if the category is popular */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_summaries" (
  "summary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content summary */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content being summarized */,
  "summary_text" TEXT /* Text of the content summary */,
  "summary_length" INTEGER /* Length of the summary in characters */,
  "summary_engagement_score" INTEGER /* Score representing user engagement with the summary */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("summary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_summaries_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES content ("content_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_shares" (
  "share_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each share event */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content being shared */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who shared the content */,
  "share_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the content was shared */,
  "share_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the content has been shared */,
  "share_type" TEXT /* Type of share (e.g., social media, email) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("share_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_shares_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_shares_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES content ("content_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the content */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the content was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, like, comment) */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to access the content */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_access_logs_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES content ("content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the content source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the content source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the content source */,
  "source_icon_url" TEXT /* URL of the source icon */,
  "source_reliability_score" INTEGER /* Score representing the reliability of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_influencers" (
  "influencer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each influencer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the influencer */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information of the influencer */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the influencer has */,
  "profile_url" TEXT /* URL of the influencer's profile */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL of the influencer's profile picture */,
  "engagement_score" INTEGER /* Score representing the influencer's engagement with the platform */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the influencer is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("influencer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  "is_trending" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tag is trending */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_tag_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content-tag mapping */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tag associated with the content */,
  "is_primary_tag" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tag is the primary tag for the content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_tag_mapping_tag_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_id") REFERENCES content_tags ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_tag_mapping_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES content ("content_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the comment was made */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the comment has received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the comment */,
  "is_flagged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the comment has been flagged for review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_comments_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES content ("content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who will receive the notification */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text of the notification */,
  "send_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., content update, new follower) */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
